A2.note: Three baskets present with real example bills.

B1.note: Flesch-Kincaid grade ~7.6; all policy terms explained inline at first use.

B3.note: Plain topics readable without legal training.

B4.note: How-a-bill-moves primer on page 1 (verified in render).

C1.note: Exactly 2 US Letter pages, re-verified with headless Chrome after fix.

C2.note: Page 1 carries stat strip, primer, three baskets, caution.

C3.note: Detail in appendices A-G (~48 pages); front brief points to them.

D1.note: All 24 cited bill keys resolve in evidence-pack.json; example bills present in Appendix A.

D2.note: All vote counts match evidence pack; Q4 imprecision fixed in-pass (see fixes).

D4.note: N/A - no committee vote counts shown anywhere in the brief.

E1.note: No should/must/recommend/urge directed at citizens or legislature.

E4.note: 2025 list framed as facts, not endorsements; saturation raised only as a question.

F2.note: Appendices A-F exist (plus G, README, appendices-print.html).

F5.note: Eyebrow header, terracotta section headers, stat strip, history example cards, baskets table, question grid, info card pair all present.

F6.note: 6-gram shingle check vs Phase 2 sample PDF: no copied headings/body; only overlap is the official issue title from config.

fixes_made.before: Every bill about who checks the State Engineer failed — six bills since 2021. The newest two won big floor votes first.
fixes_made.after: Every bill about who checks the State Engineer failed — six bills since 2021. Two recent ones (2023, 2025) won floor votes in one house before dying in the other.
fixes_made.reason: The two newest theme-8 bills are both 2025 (SB31, AB419) and AB419 never had a floor vote; the floor-vote winners were 2023 AB387 (26-14) and 2025 SB31 (15-6). 'Big' overstated 65%/71% margins. New wording matches evidence-pack.json and reality-map.json exactly.

notes_for_human_reviewers: Stat strip: 45 became law + 40 did not finish = 85 of 88; the other 3 are enrolled joint resolutions (do not become statutes), documented in Appendix F item 9 but not on the front pages.
Artifact page images refreshed after the fix; both pages are in the Phase 2 visual family (navy/terracotta on white, Arial).

designed_pages_check.page_1: Phase 2 family confirmed: THE FORUM eyebrow + navy rule, terracotta all-caps headers, navy stat cards, quote cards with terracotta top bars, navy-header baskets table. No purple.
designed_pages_check.page_2: Phase 2 family confirmed: terracotta headers, info card pair, numbered question grid, footline. No purple.
